사도행전 25:1.content: Festus therefore, having come into the province, after three days went up to Jerusalem from Caesarea.

사도행전 25:2.content: Then the high priest and the principal men of the Jews informed him against Paul, and they begged him,

사도행전 25:3.content: asking a favor against him, that he would summon him to Jerusalem; plotting to kill him on the way.

사도행전 25:4.content: However Festus answered that Paul should be kept in custody at Caesarea, and that he himself was about to depart shortly.

사도행전 25:5.content: “Let them therefore”, he said, “that are in power among you go down with me, and if there is anything wrong in the man, let them accuse him.”

사도행전 25:6.content: When he had stayed among them more than ten days, he went down to Caesarea, and on the next day he sat on the judgment seat, and commanded Paul to be brought.

사도행전 25:7.content: When he had come, the Jews who had come down from Jerusalem stood around him, bringing against him many and grievous charges which they could not prove,

사도행전 25:8.content: while he said in his defense, “Neither against the law of the Jews, nor against the temple, nor against Caesar, have I sinned at all.”

사도행전 25:9.content: But Festus, desiring to gain favor with the Jews, answered Paul and said, “Are you willing to go up to Jerusalem, and be judged by me there concerning these things?”

사도행전 25:10.content: But Paul said, “I am standing before Caesar’s judgment seat, where I ought to be tried. I have done no wrong to the Jews, as you also know very well.

사도행전 25:11.content: For if I have done wrong and have committed anything worthy of death, I don’t refuse to die; but if none of those things is true that they accuse me of, no one can give me up to them. I appeal to Caesar!”

사도행전 25:12.content: Then Festus, when he had conferred with the council, answered, “You have appealed to Caesar. To Caesar you shall go.”

사도행전 25:13.content: Now when some days had passed, King Agrippa and Bernice arrived at Caesarea, and greeted Festus.

사도행전 25:14.content: As he stayed there many days, Festus laid Paul’s case before the king, saying, “There is a certain man left a prisoner by Felix;

사도행전 25:15.content: about whom, when I was at Jerusalem, the chief priests and the elders of the Jews informed me, asking for a sentence against him.

사도행전 25:16.content: I answered them that it is not the custom of the Romans to give up any man to destruction before the accused has met the accusers face to face and has had opportunity to make his defense concerning the matter laid against him.

사도행전 25:17.content: When therefore they had come together here, I didn’t delay, but on the next day sat on the judgment seat and commanded the man to be brought.

사도행전 25:18.content: When the accusers stood up, they brought no charges against him of such things as I supposed;

사도행전 25:19.content: but had certain questions against him about their own religion, and about one Jesus, who was dead, whom Paul affirmed to be alive.

사도행전 25:20.content: Being perplexed how to inquire concerning these things, I asked whether he was willing to go to Jerusalem and there be judged concerning these matters.

사도행전 25:21.content: But when Paul had appealed to be kept for the decision of the emperor, I commanded him to be kept until I could send him to Caesar.”

사도행전 25:22.content: Agrippa said to Festus, “I also would like to hear the man myself.” “Tomorrow,” he said, “you shall hear him.”

사도행전 25:23.content: So on the next day, when Agrippa and Bernice had come with great pomp, and they had entered into the place of hearing with the commanding officers and the principal men of the city, at the command of Festus, Paul was brought in.

사도행전 25:24.content: Festus said, “King Agrippa, and all men who are here present with us, you see this man about whom all the multitude of the Jews petitioned me, both at Jerusalem and here, crying that he ought not to live any longer.

사도행전 25:25.content: But when I found that he had committed nothing worthy of death, and as he himself appealed to the emperor I determined to send him,

사도행전 25:26.content: of whom I have no certain thing to write to my lord. Therefore I have brought him out before you, and especially before you, King Agrippa, that, after examination, I may have something to write.

사도행전 25:27.content: For it seems to me unreasonable, in sending a prisoner, not to also specify the charges against him.”
